title: filetia glabra
intro: Filetia glabra is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the shrubby mint or hairy mint. It is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been found to have numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Filetia glabra can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat various ailments, including fever, colds, and skin conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
